.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:569:{"acceptance":"A single per-project land coordinator goroutine owns all writes to target refs. Unit tests in cli/internal/agent/ (or cli/internal/server/) prove: (1) happy path — worker submits base=A result=B, coordinator fetches current tip X, rebases B onto X, fast-forwards, asserts the new tip is the rebased commit and no merge commits exist in the history between A and the new tip (use git log --merges --count to assert 0); (2) rebase conflict path — worker submits a commit that cannot rebase cleanly, coordinator preserves the submission under refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<attempt-id> and returns a "preserved" status, target branch is unchanged; (3) serialization path — 5 concurrent submissions against the same project against base=A produce 5 sequential rebased commits on the target branch with linear history (git log --merges returns 0), and the coordinator processes them in order; (4) server runWorker integration — a server-managed worker whose task produces a commit B actually advances the target branch to a rebased B, via the coordinator path, visible in git log main -1 after the worker exits; (5) --local mode integration — the --local execute-loop path runs a process-local coordinator that provides the same semantics. Secondary acceptance: RealOrchestratorGitOps.Merge is deleted from execute_bead_orchestrator.go OR clearly marked as the legacy path to remove; no production code path calls git merge --no-edit on the target branch; FEAT-006, API-001, FEAT-012 still describe the same contract (rebase + ff-only, linear history). Operator-visible smoke: running ddx agent execute-loop --harness claude --model sonnet against a seeded queue produces a linear history on main with no merge commits between the run's first and last commit.","archived_at":"2026-05-07T04:29:14Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T14:35:57.684173463Z","description":"Today's execute-loop orchestration is a pile of patches on a flawed mental model. The user's target model is "human picks PR off the queue → rebases onto current tip → merges → next worker starts from the new tip." The code implements "worker branches off whatever main was when it started → runs task → git merge --no-edit that result back → 2-parent merge commit fan." The two models are incompatible; no patch to the second model gets you to the first.

Evidence the current model is wrong:

1. FEAT-006 §"Landing" (line 414), API-001 §"Landing & Preservation" (lines 217-222), and FEAT-012 §"Execute-Bead Git Operations" (line 413) all explicitly require rebase + fast-forward with linear history. The code in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:61 Merge() runs `git merge --no-edit` with a defensive `chore: checkpoint before merge` commit — zero rebase, zero ff-only, zero fetch, zero push. Three spec files aligned on one model; code implements a different one.

2. cli/internal/server/workers.go:runWorker (around line 237) calls agent.ExecuteBead via an inline ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc closure at workers.go:248 and never calls LandBeadResult. LandBeadResult is only wired into two call sites: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1372 (the `--local` execute-loop path) and cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:134 (the interactive single-bead CLI). The default `ddx agent execute-loop` path (which submits to the server) produces worker commits that are NEVER landed on main. Commits sit in worktree refs and the loop reports "success" to the tracker anyway.

3. The "worker simplification" commit 49c3d2b that closed ddx-679d2fc7 moved Merge/UpdateRef out of execute_bead.go's GitOps interface into a new OrchestratorGitOps in execute_bead_orchestrator.go. That is a code-level function split, not a process-level split. The "parent orchestrator" runs synchronously in the worker process from whichever call site reaches it first. Two workers racing on the same projectRoot will both call LandBeadResult with stale baseRev assumptions; the current Merge implementation papers over conflicts with ort-strategy merge commits. The user was explicit in their feedback: "we may not be separating concerns appropriately — we have pushed too much functionality into execute-loop and it's causing us issues; it needs to be very simple: 'I've been handed a worktree and a task, I will execute that task and make commits, produce progress output, notify done.'" The current code does not reflect that intent.

4. Six open child beads of ddx-b7ee0d6f (bd42f640 fetch-before-claim, 6aa50e57 rebase-instead-of-merge, 83ec1430 push-on-land, 2791dc4f tracker concurrency, 42dd982c divergence monitoring, b0374901 multi-machine docs) each correctly identify one piece of the gap but do not compose into a coherent end-to-end workflow. The fix is not "implement all six"; the fix is to adopt the human-PR model as the single source of truth and then let those beads fall out as sub-tasks of a coherent design.

## Target design

The human-PR mental model, encoded as code:

1. **Worker.** Accepts a pre-made worktree and a prompt. Runs the agent harness. Emits structured progress events. Produces zero or more commits in the worktree. Exits with a simple terminal status (task_succeeded / task_failed / task_no_changes) and reports its worktree branch + commit SHAs to the server. The worker NEVER fetches, rebases, merges, pushes, or writes a ref. The worker NEVER interacts with target refs on the shared project repo. The worker does not care what main is doing.

2. **Land coordinator.** A single goroutine per project (hosted on the ddx server) that owns all writes to the project's target refs. Workers submit their completed worktree branch SHAs to the coordinator via a server endpoint. The coordinator, for each submission, in order: (a) `git fetch origin <target-branch>` to refresh the target tip; (b) `git rebase --onto <target-tip> <base-rev> <result-rev>` to replay the worker's commits onto the latest tip; (c) if the rebase is clean, `git push --ff-only origin <target-branch>` (or equivalent local update-ref if no remote); (d) on rebase conflict or push rejection, move the submission to `refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<attempt-id>` and mark the bead as needing human review. The coordinator handles one submission at a time. No locks needed internally because there's only one goroutine.

3. **Worker-to-coordinator handoff.** Workers submit to a new `POST /api/agent/workers/:id/land-submission` endpoint with {worktree_branch, base_rev, result_rev, bead_id}. The endpoint enqueues the submission onto the land coordinator's channel and returns 202 Accepted. Workers block (via long-polling GET, SSE, or channel-await) for the land result — success (pushed to target), preserved (moved to iteration ref), or failed (rejected, worker should retry from new tip).

4. **Worker isolation.** Workers run in /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/ worktrees per the alpha14 worktree-relocation fix. They fetch their starting base from the coordinator ("what is the current tip?"), branch off it, work, and submit. They never touch the shared project .git beyond their own worktree's read-fetch.

5. **Tracker (beads.jsonl) coordination.** Separate concern from the git target refs. ddx-2791dc4f stays as its own bead because the tracker is a second shared resource with its own write-serialization requirement. The land coordinator and the tracker lock must both be respected, but they are orthogonal locks.

6. **Multi-machine.** When the server grows to multi-machine topology, each machine runs its own land coordinator against its local clone of the project. Coordinators push to a common origin. The origin becomes the single source of truth for the target branch. Rebase-then-push gives the per-machine coordinators a clean serialization point via origin's ref update (push is atomic at the remote).

## What this replaces

- ddx-bd42f640 (fetch+ancestry check before claim) → subsumed. The land coordinator owns the fetch; workers don't need to check ancestry because they're handed a branch from the coordinator.
- ddx-6aa50e57 (rebase instead of merge) → subsumed. The coordinator's rebase step is the canonical implementation.
- ddx-83ec1430 (push-to-origin on landing) → subsumed. Push is part of the coordinator's sequence.
- ddx-42dd982c (divergence monitoring) → reframed. Becomes monitoring of the coordinator's rejection rate and conflict rate.
- ddx-b0374901 (multi-machine topology docs) → reframed. Document the per-machine coordinator + push-to-origin model.
- The missing "server runWorker never lands" bug (new P0 child, filed alongside) → subsumed. The new model HAS a land step by design.

What this does NOT replace:

- ddx-2791dc4f (beads.jsonl concurrent-write safety) — separate concern, separate lock.
- ddx-679d2fc7 (simplify worker to task-only) — this bead is nominally closed by commit 49c3d2b, but that commit did a cosmetic function split, not the architectural process split. Re-open or file a follow-up that cleans up the nomenclature ("OrchestratorGitOps" → "CoordinatorGitOps" scoped to the single coordinator goroutine) and deletes Merge() in favor of Land(). OR leave it closed and track that cleanup under this meta-bead.

## Why this is better

The core insight: **target-ref writes must be serialized through a single writer that always sees the current tip.** That is how every real PR queue works (GitHub merge queue, gerrit, Zuul). The current design has every worker trying to be its own merge-master; the fix is to have exactly one merge-master per project and let workers be contributors.

## Rollout

This is a redesign, not a patch. Rollout steps:

1. Land a new Land(projectRoot, baseRev, resultRev, targetBranch) coordinator function that implements fetch → rebase → ff-push → preserve-on-fail. Unit tests that cover the happy path, the rebase conflict path, and the concurrent-submission serialization path.
2. Add POST /api/agent/workers/:id/land-submission + the land coordinator goroutine + per-project queue channel to cli/internal/server/workers.go.
3. Rewrite workers.go:runWorker to have the worker's executor closure submit to the local coordinator (via channel; no HTTP round-trip needed since it's in the same process) instead of calling LandBeadResult inline.
4. Delete or mothball the current LandBeadResult and its "orchestrator" naming. The new coordinator replaces it.
5. Leave --local mode working by having it instantiate a process-local single-goroutine coordinator at startup. --local remains useful for single-bead debugging.
6. Add a --coordinator-only server mode for the multi-machine eventual topology (out of scope for the first cut; reference ddx-e2f497c3 for the design).

Out of scope for this bead (but related):

- Interactive conflict resolution UI. The meta-bead says "preserve under refs/ddx/iterations/" and move on; a separate UI flow for human merge-conflict resolution is its own work.
- Cross-machine coordinators talking to each other. Out of scope; origin push suffices.
- PR approval / review gates before the coordinator lands. Separate feature.","id":"ddx-8746d8a6","issue_type":"epic","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-43009cc","status":"closed","title":"execute-loop: adopt human-PR landing model end-to-end (meta redesign)","updated_at":"2026-04-14T15:26:48.420152946Z"}

docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:189:1. **Reframe attempt** — dispatch a strong-tier reframer agent (ADR-024 P3; TD-031 §4 (Auto-Recovery Role Catalogue)) with `MinPower` set above the exhausted escalation ceiling. The reframer receives the bead record (description, AC, governing artifact refs) and returns structured edits. If the reframer returns edits, DDx applies them via `ddx bead update` paths, records `reframe-applied` (TD-027 §13), resets `consecutive_ladder_exhaustions` to 0, releases the claim, and returns the bead to `status=open` execution-ready. The bead re-enters the standard drain cycle with a fresh ladder.
docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:190:2. **Decompose attempt** (only when reframe returned no change or the reframer invocation failed) — dispatch a strong-tier decomposer agent with the same `MinPower` floor. The decomposer returns 2–5 executable child bead specs when the bead can be split under itself. If child depth is exhausted, it returns sibling or replacement bead specs under the nearest safe parent/root. DDx calls `Store.Create` for each generated bead, wires the required dependency or supersession edges, records `decompose-applied` (TD-027 §13), and leaves the oversized bead `status=open` with `Extra["execution-eligible"]=false` when it should no longer be claimed directly. The queue advances through the generated executable work.
docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:191:3. **Final escape** (only when child decomposition and sibling/replacement decomposition returned no executable work or failed) — DDx records `auto-recovery-failed` (TD-027 §13), moves the bead to `status=proposed`, clears the active claim, and does not schedule another attempt. The operator must resolve the bead via `ddx bead update --status open` (after fixing it) or `ddx bead update --status cancelled`.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel e

.ddx/beads.jsonl:19:{"created_at":"2026-05-11T19:59:02.143711599Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-0ab16765","depends_on_id":"ddx-c6317784","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T19:59:02Z"}],"description":"# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 7 migration-tooling methods used by `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` and related admin commands: `MigrateLifecycle`, `MigrateFromHelix`, `MigrateToAxon`, `MigrateDryRun`, `MigrateLifecycleDryRun`, `DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired`, `ReconcileLifecycleMetadata`. These are admin/one-shot tooling, not steady-state Backend operations. After the BL bead moves concrete `*Store` to `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/`, `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` cannot reach these methods. They need an exposure path that doesn't violate the boundary lockdown.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes the 7 migration methods. `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` imports `cli/internal/bead` and calls them on `*Store` concretely. Per TD-027 §11.5 + Migration row in the mutation rules table, these are admin tools not on the `Backend` interface. After BL, the only public surface in `cli/internal/bead/` is what's exported there — concrete `*Store` won't be reachable.

# PROPOSED FIX

Per the converged design recommendation (separate factory rather than `BeadMigration` sub-interface on Backend, to avoid forcing every backend to stub source-specific migrations):

1. Create `cli/internal/bead/migrator.go` with a `Migrator` interface and `NewMigrator` factory:
   ```go
   type Migrator interface {
       MigrateLifecycle(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       MigrateLifecycleDryRun(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       MigrateFromHelix(ctx context.Context, src string) error
       MigrateToAxon(ctx context.Context, opts MigrateToAxonOptions) error  // deprecated; replaced by ddx-IMP
       MigrateDryRun(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired(ctx context.Context) (bool, MigrationReport, error)
       ReconcileLifecycleMetadata(ctx context.Context) (ReconcileReport, error)
   }

   func NewMigrator(opts MigratorOptions) (Migrator, error)
   ```
   The concrete implementation lives in `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` after BL; the public factory returns a `Migrator` interface.

2. Move the 7 methods' bodies from `*Store` (in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` and `migrate.go`) onto an unexported `*storeMigrator` type that holds a `*Store` reference and implements `Migrator`. The `NewMigrator` factory returns this.

3. Update `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` and any related admin commands:
   ```go
   mig, err := bead.NewMigrator(bead.MigratorOptions{Dir: workspace})
   if err != nil { ... }
   mig.MigrateLifecycle(ctx, opts)
   ```

4. Remove `*Store.MigrateLifecycle`, `MigrateLifecycleDryRun`, `MigrateFromHelix`, `MigrateToAxon`, `MigrateDryRun`, `DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired`, `ReconcileLifecycleMetadata` from the `*Store` public method set. Logic moves to the `*storeMigrator` type.

5. `MigrateToAxon` retained as a deprecated method on `Migrator` that returns `ErrDeprecated` with a pointer to the IMP bead's new importer. Final removal happens after IMP lands.

# NON-SCOPE
- The new JSONL→Postgres importer (replaces `MigrateToAxon`'s JSONL-writing behavior) — separate IMP bead.
- Schema-versioning migration ladder framework — deferred until first breaking change is proposed.
- Other concerns — separate beads.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `cli/internal/bead/migrator.go` declares the `Migrator` interface (7 methods, ctx-aware) and `NewMigrator(opts) (Migrator, error)` factory. Compile-time assertion: `var _ Migrator = (*storeMigrator)(nil)`.
2. `*Store` no longer exposes the 7 migration methods directly. Verified by `grep -E 'func \(s \*Store\) (MigrateLifecycle|MigrateLifecycleDryRun|MigrateFromHelix|MigrateToAxon|MigrateDryRun|DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired|ReconcileLifecycleMetadata)\b' cli/internal/bead/*.go` returning nothing.
3. `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` constructs `Migrator` via `bead.NewMigrator(...)` and invokes operations. Holds `Migrator` interface, not `*storeMigrator` concrete.
4. End-to-end CLI invocation works: `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --dry-run`, `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --apply` produce identical output to today. Test: `TestMigrator_LifecycleMigration_RoundTrip`, `TestMigrator_FromHelix_PreservesAllFields`.
5. `MigrateToAxon` on the Migrator returns `ErrDeprecated` with message pointing at the new importer (will be implemented in IMP). Test: `TestMigrator_MigrateToAxon_ReturnsDeprecated`.
6. Existing migration tests in `cli/internal/bead/migrate_test.go` continue to pass against the new Migrator-mediated path.
7. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

The relevant branch is cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137, which falls back to JSONL unless DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL is truthy, even when config has backend: axon. ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137 and .ddx/config.yaml:1-17 do not present Axon as a normal selectable backend. The config type already has a Backend field, but the factory still requires an env override and emits a warning instead of honoring configuration directly.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update the store factory so bead_tracker.backend=axon selects the Axon backend from config without requiring the experimental env var.
- Update the checked-in .ddx/config.yaml example to show the backend and Axon connection block.
- Keep the default backend unchanged when backend is unset.
- Preserve bd/br behavior for the existing external backend path.

NON-SCOPE

.ddx/beads.jsonl:92:{"created_at":"2026-05-11T19:59:00.307694448Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-3af1c1a6","depends_on_id":"ddx-c6317784","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T19:59:00Z"}],"description":"# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 3 dependency-graph methods (`DepAdd`, `DepRemove`, `DepTree`) and 3 queue-ordering methods (`QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`). Per TD-027 §6 the dep methods belong on `BeadDependencyReader`/`BeadDependencyWriter` sub-interfaces (signature-only change — ctx added). Queue ordering is operator-driven workflow; per TD-027 §14 it belongs in `ops/queue/` helpers with typed `Operation` values.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes these 6 methods. Foundation declared the `BeadDependencyReader`/`BeadDependencyWriter` interfaces but ctx isn't yet on `*Store`'s dep methods. `BeadQueue` isn't an interface in TD-027 — queue ops are workflow concerns that live as helpers.

# PROPOSED FIX

## Dependencies (signature-only)

1. Update `*Store.DepAdd(ctx, id, depID)`, `DepRemove(ctx, id, depID)`, `DepTree(ctx, rootID)` to take ctx.
2. Compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadDependencyReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadDependencyWriter = (*Store)(nil)`.
3. Thread ctx through callers. Narrow field types opportunistically (e.g., `ddx bead dep tree` CLI handler can narrow to `BeadDependencyReader`).

## Queue (helper-migration; methods removed)

4. Add named `Operation` types to `cli/internal/bead/operation.go`:
   - `QueueSetTop{}` — move bead to top of queue (sparse rank ordering).
   - `QueueSetPosition{Position int}` — operator move-to-position.
   - `QueueClearOp{}` — remove queue rank.
5. Create `cli/internal/bead/ops/queue/queue.go`:
   - `Top(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `QueueSetTop{}`.
   - `Move(ctx, l, id, position int) error` — wraps `QueueSetPosition`.
   - `Clear(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `QueueClearOp{}`.
6. Migrate callers (operator queue manipulation in `cli/cmd/bead.go` queue subcommands) from `*Store.QueueTop`/`QueueMove`/`QueueClear` → `ops/queue/` helpers.
7. Remove `*Store.QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`.

# NON-SCOPE
- Dependency cycle detection — handled at higher layer.
- Queue rank renormalization algorithm — internal to ops/queue; sparse-rank logic remains as-is.
- Other concerns — separate beads.
- Module boundary lockdown — `ddx-74452926`.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `*Store.DepAdd`, `DepRemove`, `DepTree` take ctx. Compile-time assertions for `BeadDependencyReader` and `BeadDependencyWriter` compile.
2. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` declares `QueueSetTop`, `QueueSetPosition{Position}`, `QueueClearOp` types with `Apply(b *Bead) error` impls. Tests: `TestOperation_QueueSetTop_AssignsTopRank`, `TestOperation_QueueSetPosition_RespectsBucket`, `TestOperation_QueueClearOp_RemovesRank`.
3. `cli/internal/bead/ops/queue/` package exists with helpers + tests: `TestOpsQueue_Top_MovesBeadToFront`, `TestOpsQueue_Move_PreservesPriorityBucket`, `TestOpsQueue_Clear_RemovesOperatorOverride`.
4. `*Store` no longer exposes `QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`. Verified by grep.
5. Caller files no longer reference these methods on `*Store` outside the bead package.
6. Queue-ordering invariants (SD-004 §"Ready queue" / queue derivation) preserved — operator overrides apply within a priority bucket; explicit rank wins over missing rank within bucket. Test: `TestQueueOrdering_OperatorRankWithinBucket`.
7. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels

Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel e
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX

- `*Store.Apply` method exists; type-asserts to `OperationApplier`; falls back to generic load-mutate-save. Test: `TestStore_Apply_DelegatesToOperationApplier_WhenAvailable`, `TestStore_Apply_GenericFallback_WhenNotAvailable`.
- `JSONLBackend` implements `OperationApplier` (empty switch + generic fallback — heartbeat-specific cases added in C bead). Compile-time assertion: `var _ OperationApplier = (*JSONLBackend)(nil)`. Test: `TestJSONLBackend_Apply_FallsThroughForUnknownOps`.
- Bead data model invariants (TD-027 §11.2) hold across `*Store`. Test: `TestBeadDataModel_InvariantsHold` asserts the 12 universal invariants on freshly-created and mutated beads.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:121:{"created_at":"2026-05-11T19:59:03.058900158Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-53df5e2f","depends_on_id":"ddx-9c5bca8f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T19:59:03Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-53df5e2f","depends_on_id":"ddx-0ab16765","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T19:59:03Z"}],"description":"# PROBLEM
The legacy `*Store.MigrateToAxon` method (now exposed via `Migrator.MigrateToAxon` in MIG bead, returning `ErrDeprecated`) writes JSONL files under `.ddx/axon/` rather than performing an actual JSONL→Postgres migration. Per the audit in `docs/plans/plan-2026-05-10-axon-only-architecture.md`, production AxonStore deployment requires migrating existing `.ddx/beads.jsonl` corpora into Lakebase Postgres. The real importer is unimplemented.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/migrate.go` (function previously at `*Store.MigrateToAxon`, now folded into `*storeMigrator` per MIG bead) writes JSONL into a sidecar directory. The function does not connect to Postgres or invoke `AxonStore.Apply`/`AxonStore.Create`. Production deployment cannot use it to populate Lakebase.

# PROPOSED FIX

1. Implement a real importer in `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/import_postgres.go` (or equivalent post-BL location). The importer:
   - Reads `.ddx/beads.jsonl` (and `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl` if present) — uses existing JSONL parser.
   - For each bead, calls `axonStore.Create(ctx, &b)` to insert into Postgres. Idempotent on `ErrConflict` (skip + log).
   - For each bead, appends events via `axonStore.Apply(ctx, id, AppendEventOp{...})` per TD-027 §13 vocabulary (events come from `Extra["events"]` on the JSONL bead or from sidecar `.ddx/attachments/<bead>/events.jsonl`).
   - Tracks progress, supports `--dry-run` (counts without writes), `--limit N` (batched migration).
   - Verifies post-migration: read back beads from Postgres, assert field-for-field equality with source JSONL (sample-based for large corpora).

2. Expose via `Migrator` interface (added in MIG): rename/replace `MigrateToAxon` with the real implementation; same method name, different (correct) behavior.

3. Update `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` to support the importer with appropriate flags:
   - `ddx bead migrate --to-axon --dry-run`
   - `ddx bead migrate --to-axon --apply [--limit N]`
   - `ddx bead migrate --to-axon --verify` (read-back + checksum)

4. Remove the deprecated JSONL-writing behavior. `ErrDeprecated` return from MIG bead is replaced by the real implementation.

# NON-SCOPE
- Schema-versioning migration ladder (separate concern; deferred until first v1→v2 breaking change).
- Real-wire integration test against actual Postgres — separate WIRE bead.
- Continuous sync between JSONL and Postgres — not in scope; this is one-shot migration.
- AxonStore itself — implemented in AX (`ddx-9c5bca8f`).

# Acceptance criteria

1. New file `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/import_postgres.go` (or equivalent) implements the JSONL→Postgres importer. Uses `axonStore.Create` + `axonStore.Apply` for inserts + event appends.
2. Existing `Migrator.MigrateToAxon` method returns the real importer's result (not `ErrDeprecated`).
3. CLI flags wired: `--to-axon --dry-run`, `--to-axon --apply`, `--to-axon --verify`. Test: `TestImporter_DryRun_CountsOnly`, `TestImporter_Apply_WritesToPostgres`, `TestImporter_Verify_DetectsDrift`.
4. Idempotency: re-running `--apply` on an already-imported corpus is a no-op (skips conflicts). Test: `TestImporter_Apply_Idempotent_OnReRun`.
5. Field-fidelity: every JSONL bead field is preserved in Postgres, including `extra` preserved-keys. Test: `TestImporter_PreservesExtras`.
6. Event ordering: events imported preserve monotonic `created_at` (TD-027 §11.2 invariant 8). Test: `TestImporter_PreservesEventOrdering`.
7. Attachment migration: sidecar files under `.ddx/attachments/<bead>/` are uploaded to Unity Catalog Volumes (or local-equivalent for testing). Test: `TestImporter_MigratesAttachments`.
8. Verification mode: post-import, sample N beads, read back from AxonStore, assert equality with source. Test: `TestImporter_Verify_RoundTripPassesOnCleanImport`.
9. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green (with httptest-served Axon-shaped GraphQL endpoint or in-memory backend).
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:161:{"created_at":"2026-05-11T18:42:56.885339633Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-74452926","depends_on_id":"ddx-bca628fa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T20:00:38Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-74452926","depends_on_id":"ddx-e1c743d3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T20:00:38Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-74452926","depends_on_id":"ddx-f39b41b3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T20:00:38Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-74452926","depends_on_id":"ddx-aed7c7ab","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T20:00:39Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-74452926","depends_on_id":"ddx-bc07270f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T20:00:39Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-74452926","depends_on_id":"ddx-3af1c1a6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T20:00:39Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-74452926","depends_on_id":"ddx-71d3a2de","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T20:00:39Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-74452926","depends_on_id":"ddx-0ab16765","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T20:00:39Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-74452926","depends_on_id":"ddx-9c5bca8f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T20:00:40Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-74452926","depends_on_id":"ddx-29f02cf4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T20:00:40Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-74452926","depends_on_id":"ddx-900a8d38","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T20:00:40Z"}],"description":"# PROBLEM
After Phase 1 (Foundation `ddx-c6317784`) and per-concern beads (L/C/X/Q/D/A/MIG) land, callers no longer use the removed workflow methods on `*Store` — but they still hold `*Store` as a struct field type for foundational reads/writes/queries/etc. The `Backend` interface composite isn't yet asserted on `*Store`. The concrete types (`*Store`, `RawBackend`, `JSONLBackend`, `ExternalBackend`, `AxonStore`, `*WatcherHub`) live at `cli/internal/bead/` — publicly importable from any other package.

The goal of TD-027 §21 is **physical impossibility** of bypass: not lint-prevented, not review-prevented, but a Go compiler error if any code outside `cli/internal/bead/` tries to import the concrete storage implementations. This bead enforces that via Go's `internal/` rule.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go` — `JSONLBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_external.go` — `ExternalBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go` (or `AxonStore` post `ddx-9c5bca8f`) — public.
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go` — `*WatcherHub` public.
- All supporting concrete implementation files (`claim_liveness.go`, `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc.) — public.

Plus the final assembly step (formerly "K" in earlier breakdowns): `Backend` composite interface declared, `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite, compile-time assertion `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`. These were deferred from Foundation because the composite couldn't be asserted until all per-concern beads removed their methods.

# PROPOSED FIX

This bead does three load-bearing things in one atomic change:

## 1. Narrow caller field types from `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (or narrower)

Every caller file in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, `cli/internal/escalation/`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/`, `cli/internal/attemptmetrics/`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/`, `cli/internal/exec/`, `cli/internal/agent/try/` that holds `*bead.Store` as a struct field or local variable is updated:

- Most callers: change `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (full interface). Mechanical rename.
- Narrow candidates (identified during per-concern beads): use narrower sub-interface where appropriate (e.g., `cli/cmd/work_focus.go` → `bead.BeadQueries`; `cli/internal/agentmetrics/loader.go` → `bead.BeadReader`).
- Documented exceptions remain on concrete `*Store` access OR on direct file I/O (allowlisted):
  - `cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go` — file-level repair; reads `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.
  - `cli/cmd/bead.go` (git auto-commit + conflict-resolution code paths only) — operates on the file as a file.
  - `cli/cmd/sync.go` — `ddx sync` operates on the file.

## 2. Add `Backend` composite + compile-time assertion

- Declare `Backend` as the union of all 11 sub-interfaces:
  `BeadInitializer + BeadReader + BeadLifecycle + BeadEventReader + BeadEventWriter + BeadQueries + BeadDependencyReader + BeadDependencyWriter + BeadArchive + BeadInterchangeReader + BeadInterchangeWriter`.
- Declare `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite (5 reader sub-interfaces) per TD-027 §6.1.
- Add compile-time assertion: `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`.

## 3. Move concretes to internal/

- Create `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/`.
- Move (with package rename):
  - `store.go` → `internal/storage/store.go` (package `storage`)
  - `backend_jsonl.go` → `internal/storage/backend_jsonl.go`
  - `backend_external.go` → `internal/storage/backend_external.go`
  - `axon_backend.go` / `axon_store.go` → `internal/storage/`
  - `claim_liveness.go` → `internal/storage/`
  - `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc. → `internal/storage/`
  - `watcher.go` → `internal/lifecycle/watcher_hub.go` (package `lifecycle`)
  - `RawBackend` and `OperationApplier` interfaces also move into `internal/storage/`
- Create `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` as the SOLE public construction path:
  ```go
  package bead
  import "cli/internal/bead/internal/storage"
  func NewStore(opts StoreOptions) (Backend, error) { return storage.New(opts) }
  ```
- The public `cli/internal/bead/` package surface is now: `Bead` struct, `Backend` + sub-interfaces, `Operation` + named ops, errors, `IDGenerator` + `ValidateID`, ctx accessors, `LifecycleSubscriber` interface, `Migrator` factory (from MIG bead), and the `ops/` helper sub-packages.

## 4. Lint cleanup

After lockdown, two analyzers in the lint suite (`ddx-e91a45c0`) are redundant because Go's `internal/` rule enforces the same property at the compiler level:
- `concrete-store-methods` — removed.
- `no-internal-store-construction` — removed.

Retained analyzers (defense-in-depth inside `cli/internal/bead/`):
- `direct-bead-jsonl-io` (callers still shouldn't bypass the abstraction)
- `no-new-rawbackend-impls` (new backends use Backend directly)
- `typed-context-accessors-only` (ctx.Value discipline)

# NON-SCOPE
- Per-concern bead work — must complete first.
- AxonStore implementation — `ddx-9c5bca8f`.
- IMP (JSONL→Postgres importer) — separate downstream bead.
- WIRE (real-wire integration tests) — separate downstream bead.

# Acceptance criteria

1. Every caller file in scope (per PROPOSED FIX §1) has its `*bead.Store` field/variable types narrowed to `bead.Backend` or a sub-interface, with the allowlist exceptions (`bead_doctor.go`, file-level paths in `bead.go`, `sync.go`).
2. `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` declares `Backend` as the union of 11 sub-interfaces. `ReadOnlyBackend` declared per TD-027 §6.1.
3. `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/` exist; all concrete implementation files relocated with appropriate package renames.
4. `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` is the sole public construction path. Exports `NewStore`, `NewLifecycleSubscriber`, `NewMigrator` (from MIG). No other public function constructs a concrete storage type.
5. **Build must succeed**: `cd cli && go build ./...`. **Failure indicates incomplete per-concern bead migration** — diagnose the failing caller and complete its migration before retrying. Do NOT bypass via build tags or temporary public re-exports.
6. Boundary verification test: `TestModuleBoundary_NoInternalImportsOutsideBead` — static analysis scans every Go file under `cli/` outside `cli/internal/bead/` and asserts none of them import `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage` or `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle`. AST-based.
7. Compile-time `var _ Backend = (*storage.Store)(nil)` exists inside `internal/storage/` (verifying Backend satisfaction now that the concrete type is private to that package).
8. Lint cleanup: `concrete-store-methods` and `no-internal-store-construction` analyzers removed from the bead-lints binary. Documented in `cli/tools/lints/README.md`. The retained 3 analyzers stay.
9. Existing tests pass without modification. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

- No operator override behavior; ddx-cd69c24 owns idempotency.

Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 4 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel e
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Legacy format handling: beads with `schema_version` absent in JSONL read as `schema_version=1` per TD-027 §11.4. Test: `TestBead_LegacyFormat_ReadsAsV1`.
- Every caller that uses Init/ReadAll/Get/Create/Update/Close/GenID passes ctx through. No `*Store.Init()` (no-ctx) calls remain in production code outside test fixtures. Event callers are intentionally excluded and handled by E.
- `RawBackend` docstring updated with the warning text from TD-027 §6.2 directing new backends to implement `Backend` directly.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.","id":"ddx-8e729130","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:iterate","area:bead","area:storage","kind:feature","kind:refactor","spec:FEAT-004","spec:TD-027","reliability","decomposed"],"parent":"ddx-c6317784","priority":0,"status":"open","title":"F (Foundation): Operation pattern + IDGenerator + sentinels + ctx access: part 4","updated_at":"2026-05-12T19:40:28.802897344Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:205:{"created_at":"2026-05-11T17:54:48.993345328Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-900a8d38","depends_on_id":"ddx-c6317784","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T17:54:49Z"}],"description":"# PROBLEM
`*bead.WatcherHub` (`cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:36-66`) constructs its own `*Store` internally via `NewStore(projectID + "/.ddx")`. This bypasses any factory-level capability validation or backend selection. Today everything is JSONL so this is invisible; once AxonStore ships and `bead.backend: axon` is configured for production, the WatcherHub still polls a local JSONL file while the authoritative bead state lives in Lakebase Postgres. Result: silent stale-data bug — `beadLifecycle` GraphQL subscriptions return empty or stale events.

# ROOT CAUSE (file:line)
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:54-66` — `SubscribeLifecycle` instantiates a `*Store` directly: `store := NewStore(projectID + "/.ddx")`. No injection point for the configured backend.
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:60-65` — `newProjectWatcher(store, h.interval)` ties the watcher to that locally-constructed Store.
- `cli/internal/server/server.go` — constructs `*WatcherHub` and passes it to subscription resolvers. The hub is constructed once at server startup; the project Stores it creates internally are detached from any per-project factory.

# PROPOSED FIX
Change `WatcherHub` to take a `bead.BeadReader` (or `Backend`) factory function at construction:

```go
type StoreFactory func(projectID string) (bead.BeadReader, error)

type WatcherHub struct {
    factory  StoreFactory
    watchers map[string]*projectWatcher
    interval time.Duration
    ...
}

func NewWatcherHub(factory StoreFactory, interval time.Duration) *WatcherHub { ... }

func (h *WatcherHub) SubscribeLifecycle(ctx context.Context, projectID string) (<-chan LifecycleEvent, func(), error) {
    // ...
    store, err := h.factory(projectID)
    if err != nil { return nil, nil, err }
    pw = newProjectWatcher(store, h.interval)
    // ...
}
```

`cli/internal/server/server.go` is updated to pass a factory that uses the project's configured backend (selected via the same config pathway that the rest of ddx-server uses).

# NON-SCOPE
- Native subscription transport on AxonStore (separate work — would replace the polling-based `projectWatcher` with native push when available).
- Changing `LifecycleEvent` shape or semantics.
- Multi-tenant identity propagation (handled separately via the `bead.WithIdentity` ctx accessor pattern).
- Modifying callers other than `cli/internal/server/server.go` and the subscription resolver.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `WatcherHub` constructor signature changes to accept a `StoreFactory` parameter. Compile-time assertion `var _ LifecycleSubscriber = (*WatcherHub)(nil)` still holds.
2. `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go` no longer contains a direct `NewStore(...)` call.
3. `cli/internal/server/server.go` constructs `WatcherHub` with a factory that delegates to the same backend-selection path the rest of the server uses.
4. Test `TestWatcherHub_UsesProvidedFactory` injects a fake factory, asserts the factory is invoked when SubscribeLifecycle is called, and asserts the watcher polls the injected `bead.BeadReader` rather than the filesystem.
5. Test `TestWatcherHub_PropagatesFactoryErrors` injects a factory that returns an error and asserts SubscribeLifecycle returns the error.
6. Existing `TestWatcherHub_*` tests in `cli/internal/bead/watcher_test.go` are updated to use the factory pattern; they continue to pass.

The package-level conformance coverage still needs a single matrix that proves both backends satisfy the same behavioral contract. The current chaos harness already names jsonl and axon in cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go:23-36, but the rest of the package still relies on ad hoc store construction and has no package-wide backend matrix to prove the new Axon path behaves identically.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go:23-36 only defines the backend table; there is no end-to-end package test matrix yet that runs the conformance suite through both backends and keeps the existing package tests honest. The rest of the test package still defaults to newTestStore(t) in many places, so the Axon path is not yet a first-class test target.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extend the backend test helpers so the package conformance suite can run under both jsonl and axon.
- Keep TestChaos_ConcurrentAppendSafety, TestChaos_AtomicStatusTransitions, TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseAndAppend, and TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseNotLost as explicit backend matrix coverage.
- Make the package test harness clearly fail if either backend diverges.

NON-SCOPE
- GraphQL client generation.
- Backend selection/config plumbing.
- Subscription integration smoke tests.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"codex","resolved_model":"gpt-5.4-mini","fallback_chain":[],"actual_power":8}","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=codex model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260506T120844-d2a3f066","harness":"codex","model":"gpt-5.4-mini","input_tokens":1670655,"output_tokens":10807,"total_tokens":1681462,"cost_usd":0,"duration_ms":168678,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=1681462 model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"escalation_count":0,"fallback_chain":[],"final_tier":"","requested_profile":"","requested_tier":"","resolved_model":"gpt-5.4-mini","resolved_provider":"codex","resolved_tier":""}","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"provider=codex model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"erik","body":"failure_class=review-error: transport

The current Axon backend does not talk to Axon at all; it rewrites local JSONL snapshots under <dir>/axon/ instead of using the GraphQL entity store described by TD-030. That is visible in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215, where AxonBackend stores beads/events as local files and serializes them with writeAtomicFile instead of remote operations.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215 implements the backend as file-backed emulation, not a GraphQL-backed store. This bypasses the typed client, bypasses optimistic concurrency against Axon, and makes the backend selectable only in a fake local mode.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace local snapshot writes with real GraphQL CRUD, dependency, and event mutations using cli/internal/bead/axon/.
- Preserve the two-collection model from TD-030: ddx_beads and ddx_bead_events, with event_of links.
- Keep the existing backend interface surface working for Create, Get, Update, Claim, Close, DepAdd, DepRemove, AppendEvent, ExportTo, and Import.
- Preserve jsonl as the default backend path so day-1 behavior does not change.

NON-SCOPE
- Backend selection config plumbing.
- Package test matrix broadening.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:257:{"created_at":"2026-05-11T19:58:58.437857637Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-aed7c7ab","depends_on_id":"ddx-c6317784","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T19:58:58Z"}],"description":"# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 6 cancel/cooldown methods (`RequestCancel`, `IsCancelRequested`, `MarkCancelHonored`, `SetExecutionCooldown`, `ClearCooldowns`, `IncrNoChangesCount`). Cancel signals cooperative shutdown; cooldown parks beads from re-execution after failures. Per TD-027 §11.5 + §14 these belong in `ops/cancel/` and `ops/cooldown/` helpers + typed `Operation` values. Callers are in `cli/internal/escalation/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, and the operator CLI.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes these 6 methods. The drain loop's cooldown policy (TD-031 §2 outcome mapping) writes via `SetExecutionCooldown`; the escalation policy in `cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go` reads + manipulates cooldown state. Cancel signals are written by operators via the CLI and read by the worker's mid-attempt check.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §11.5 + §14 for cancel + cooldown:

1. Add named `Operation` types to `cli/internal/bead/operation.go`:
   - `SetCancelRequested{At time.Time}` — sets `cancel.requested=true` + `cancel.requested_at`.
   - `ClearCancelRequested{}` — clears both fields.
   - `MarkCancelHonoredOp{At time.Time}` — sets `cancel.honored=true` + `cancel.honored_at`.
   - `SetCooldown{Until time.Time; Status, Detail, BaseRev string}`.
   - `ClearCooldownOp{}` — clears cooldown fields.
   - `IncrNoChangesCount{}` — atomic increment via `Apply`.
2. Create `cli/internal/bead/ops/cancel/cancel.go`:
   - `Request(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `SetCancelRequested{At: time.Now()}`.
   - `Clear(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `ClearCancelRequested{}`.
   - `MarkHonored(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `MarkCancelHonoredOp{At: time.Now()}`.
   - `IsRequested(b *Bead) bool` — pure predicate.
3. Create `cli/internal/bead/ops/cooldown/cooldown.go`:
   - `Set(ctx, l, id, opts SetOptions) error` — wraps `SetCooldown`.
   - `Clear(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `ClearCooldownOp{}`.
   - `ClearAll(ctx, l BeadLifecycle, q BeadQueries, filter func(*Bead) bool) (int, error)` — iterates with `Apply`.
   - `IncrNoChanges(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `IncrNoChangesCount{}`.
   - `IsActive(b *Bead, now time.Time) bool` — pure predicate (per TD-027 §11.2 invariant 6: `cooldown.until` in past → no-op).
4. Migrate callers:
   - `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead*.go` — cancel checks + cooldown setting.
   - `cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go` — cooldown set/clear.
   - `cli/cmd/work_clear_cooldowns.go` — operator clear-all.
   - Any test files asserting cancel/cooldown workflow behavior → use helpers.
5. Remove `*Store.RequestCancel`, `IsCancelRequested`, `MarkCancelHonored`, `SetExecutionCooldown`, `ClearCooldowns`, `IncrNoChangesCount` (6 methods).

# NON-SCOPE
- Cooldown retry-window policy (15-minute ceiling, which outcomes may set retry-after) — TD-031 §2.1 owns that; storage exposes the field.
- Cancellation acknowledgment semantics — workflow concern; helper provides the field-level mutation.
- Other concerns (lifecycle, claim, etc.) — separate beads.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` declares the 6 cancel/cooldown Operation types. Each implements `Apply(b *Bead) error`. Tests: `TestOperation_SetCancelRequested_SetsFields`, `TestOperation_MarkCancelHonored_RequiresPriorRequest`, `TestOperation_IncrNoChangesCount_Increments`, `TestOperation_SetCooldown_PersistsAllFields`.
2. `cli/internal/bead/ops/cancel/` and `cli/internal/bead/ops/cooldown/` packages exist with helpers + tests: `TestOpsCancel_Request_RoundTrip`, `TestOpsCancel_IsRequested_Predicate`, `TestOpsCooldown_Set_PersistsAllFields`, `TestOpsCooldown_ClearAll_FiltersCorrectly`, `TestOpsCooldown_IsActive_RespectsPastUntil`.
3. `*Store` no longer exposes the 6 cancel/cooldown methods.
4. Caller files no longer reference these on `*Store` (verified by grep).
5. Escalation flow continues to set/clear cooldowns correctly. Test: `TestEscalation_NoChangesUnverified_SetsCooldown_ViaHelper`.
6. TD-027 §11.2 invariant 6 verified: `cooldown.until` in past → queue derivation ignores cooldown. Test: `TestCooldown_PastUntil_QueueIgnoresIt`.
7. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

`*Store` exposes 12 query methods (`List`, `ListWithArchive`, `Ready`, `ReadyExecution`, `ReadyExecutionBreakdown`, `ProposedOperatorAttention`, `NeedsHuman`, `Blocked`, `ExternalBlocked`, `DependencyWaiting`, `BlockedAll`, `Status`) that compute derived views over the bead corpus. Per TD-027 §6.1 these compose into `BeadQueries` interface; per AC #1 every interface method takes `ctx context.Context`. Several callers (`cli/cmd/work_focus.go`, `cli/internal/agent/preview_queue.go`, `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_beads.go`) are clean ISP narrowing candidates — they use queries only.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` (and related files for derivation logic) defines these 12 methods without ctx. The Foundation bead declared `BeadQueries` interface (ctx-aware) but the `*Store` methods don't yet match.

# PROPOSED FIX
1. Update all 12 query methods on `*Store` to take `ctx context.Context` as first parameter.
2. Add compile-time assertion: `var _ BeadQueries = (*Store)(nil)`.
3. Thread ctx through every caller of these methods. Many callers can also narrow their `*bead.Store` field type to `bead.BeadQueries` (ISP win) — opportunistic narrowing in this bead; full narrowing pass in BL.
4. No method removal — these methods stay on `*Store`; they ARE the storage primitives for derived views (no Operation pattern needed; pure reads).

# NON-SCOPE
- Queue derivation logic (TD-027 §2.1) — not modified.
- Pagination / cursor support for List — deferred (TD-027 §25 open question).
- Other concerns — separate beads.
- Module boundary lockdown — `ddx-74452926`.

# Acceptance criteria

1. All 12 query methods on `*Store` take `ctx context.Context` as first parameter. Compile-time assertion `var _ BeadQueries = (*Store)(nil)` compiles.
2. Caller files updated to pass ctx. Test: `TestBeadQueries_*_AcceptsContext` (parameterized over each method).
3. At least 3 caller files narrowed from `*bead.Store` to `bead.BeadQueries` field type. Concrete:
   - `cli/cmd/work_focus.go` (uses `BlockedAll`, `List`, `ProposedOperatorAttention`, `ReadyExecution` — pure queries).
   - `cli/internal/agent/preview_queue.go` (uses `ReadyExecution`).
   - `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_beads.go` (uses `DependencyWaiting`, `ExternalBlocked`, `Ready`, `Status` — pure read-side resolver).
   - `cli/internal/agentmetrics/loader.go` (uses `ReadAll` — strong reader narrowing candidate; BeadReader, not Queries, but related).
4. Existing query tests in `cli/internal/bead/` continue to pass.
5. Ctx cancellation propagates: if ctx is cancelled mid-query, the method returns `ctx.Err()`. Test: `TestBeadQueries_RespectsContextCancellation`.
6. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:277:{"created_at":"2026-05-11T19:58:55.438930116Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-bca628fa","depends_on_id":"ddx-c6317784","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T19:58:56Z"}],"description":"# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 7 lifecycle-transition methods (`TransitionLifecycle`, `SetLifecycleStatus`, `UpdateWithLifecycleStatus`, `CloseWithEvidence`, `AppendNotes`, `Reopen`, `ParkToProposed`) that encode HELIX-flavored state-machine policy directly in the storage layer. Callers in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, and `cli/internal/server/graphql/` hold `*Store` concretely and invoke these methods. Per TD-027's storage-vs-workflow separation, the state-machine validation belongs in a workflow helper package, with the storage primitive being `Apply(ctx, id, op Operation)` plus typed `Operation` values.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes lifecycle methods that mutate status with HELIX-specific transition validation embedded. `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_beads.go`, `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead*.go`, and `cli/cmd/bead.go` call these methods directly. The new `Operation` pattern (added in Foundation bead `ddx-c6317784`) provides the primitive but the workflow helpers don't exist yet.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §14 helper package `cli/internal/bead/ops/lifecycle/`:

1. Add named `Operation` types to `cli/internal/bead/operation.go`: `SetStatus{Status}`, `SetLifecycleStatus{Status, Options}`, `SetCloseEvidence{SessionID, CommitSHA}`, `ReopenOp{Reason, Notes}`, `ParkToProposedOp{Reason}`, `AppendNotes{Notes}`.
2. Create `cli/internal/bead/ops/lifecycle/transition.go` with helpers:
   - `Transition(ctx, l BeadLifecycle, r BeadReader, id, newStatus string) error` — validates against TD-027 §2 transition matrix; calls `l.Apply(ctx, id, SetLifecycleStatus{Status: newStatus})`.
   - `CloseWithEvidence(ctx, l, id, sessionID, commitSHA string) error`.
   - `Reopen(ctx, l, id, reason, notes string) error`.
   - `ParkToProposed(ctx, l, id, reason string) error`.
   - `AppendNotes(ctx, l, id, notes string) error`.
   - `SetStatus(ctx, l, id, status string) error` — for callers that don't need state-machine validation.
3. Migrate callers:
   - `cli/cmd/bead.go` — `store.Reopen(...)`, `store.CloseWithEvidence(...)`, `store.UpdateWithLifecycleStatus(...)` → `lifecycle.Reopen(ctx, store, ...)` etc.
   - `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_beads.go` — `store.TransitionLifecycle`, `store.UpdateWithLifecycleStatus`, `store.Reopen` → helper calls.
   - `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead*.go` — lifecycle transition usages → helpers.
   - All test files that use these methods (where they assert workflow behavior) → helpers; tests that assert storage primitive behavior stay using `Apply` directly.
4. Remove `*Store.TransitionLifecycle`, `SetLifecycleStatus`, `UpdateWithLifecycleStatus`, `CloseWithEvidence`, `AppendNotes`, `Reopen`, `ParkToProposed` (7 methods).

# NON-SCOPE
- Other concerns (claim, cancel, cooldown, queries, dependency, archive) — separate beads.
- Module boundary lockdown — bead `ddx-74452926`.
- Changes to the state machine itself (statuses, transitions) — defined in TD-027 §1–§2; not modified here.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` declares the 6 lifecycle Operation types listed in PROPOSED FIX. Each implements `Apply(b *Bead) error`. Test: `TestOperation_LifecycleOps_AllImplementApply` (reflection-enumerated).
2. `cli/internal/bead/ops/lifecycle/` package exists with the helper functions. Each helper has at least one test against `*Store`: `TestOpsLifecycle_Transition_HappyPath`, `TestOpsLifecycle_Transition_RejectsInvalidTransition`, `TestOpsLifecycle_CloseWithEvidence_RecordsProvenance`, `TestOpsLifecycle_Reopen_FromClosedFails` (closed is terminal), `TestOpsLifecycle_ParkToProposed_AppendsTriageEvent`.
3. `*Store` no longer exposes the 7 lifecycle methods. Compile-time check: `grep -E 'func \(s \*Store\) (TransitionLifecycle|SetLifecycleStatus|UpdateWithLifecycleStatus|CloseWithEvidence|AppendNotes|Reopen|ParkToProposed)\b' cli/internal/bead/*.go` returns nothing.
4. Caller files no longer reference these methods on `*Store`. Verified by `grep -rln 'store\.\(TransitionLifecycle\|SetLifecycleStatus\|UpdateWithLifecycleStatus\|CloseWithEvidence\|AppendNotes\|Reopen\|ParkToProposed\)' cli/` outside the bead package and test fixtures, which returns zero matches.
5. Existing test behavior preserved: `cli/internal/bead/store_test.go`, `chaos_test.go`, `backend_conformance_test.go` continue to pass (they test storage primitives, not the workflow helpers).
6. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:295:{"created_at":"2026-05-11T19:59:04.274169077Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-c479688b","depends_on_id":"ddx-9c5bca8f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T19:59:04Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-c479688b","depends_on_id":"ddx-53df5e2f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T19:59:05Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-c479688b","depends_on_id":"ddx-958b8fc3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T19:59:05Z"}],"description":"# PROBLEM
The conformance suite (`ddx-958b8fc3`) parameterizes tests across `*Store` and `AxonStore`, but `AxonStore` is tested against an httptest-served Axon-shaped GraphQL endpoint — a mock. Production AxonStore deployment requires verifying behavior against an actual Postgres-backed Axon service. Without real-wire tests, semantic differences (timestamp precision, transaction isolation, NULL handling, etc.) can silently diverge between the mock and prod.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/axon/` and the conformance suite use mocked transports. No CI job runs AxonStore against real Postgres. Per `docs/plans/plan-2026-05-10-axon-only-architecture.md`'s audit, "Zero tests run against Postgres" was a gap. The current state remains gap-ful for production verification.

# PROPOSED FIX
1. Add a real-wire integration test package under `cli/internal/bead/integration/axon/`. Each test:
   - Provisions a Postgres instance (via Docker testcontainer, `dockertest`, or similar in-CI Postgres).
   - Starts an Axon service against that Postgres (or uses Axon's standalone test mode).
   - Constructs `AxonStore` via the real GraphQL transport pointing at the Axon service.
   - Runs the existing conformance suite + Axon-specific tests against the real backend.

2. Test cases (in addition to the parameterized conformance):
   - `TestAxonStore_RealPostgres_ClaimCAS_AtomicUnderConcurrency` — 10 concurrent claims; assert exactly one succeeds, others get `ErrAlreadyClaimed`.
   - `TestAxonStore_RealPostgres_HeartbeatNativeUpdate` — assert `SetClaimHeartbeat` produces a single `UPDATE` SQL statement (no full-row read-modify-write); verify via Postgres query logs.
   - `TestAxonStore_RealPostgres_EventAppend_Monotonic` — concurrent event appends preserve TD-027 §11.2 invariant 8 (monotonic per bead).
   - `TestAxonStore_RealPostgres_TransactionIsolation` — `Apply` operations are atomic under SERIALIZABLE / READ COMMITTED (whatever Lakebase default is).
   - `TestAxonStore_RealPostgres_NullPreservation` — `omitempty` JSONL fields map correctly to Postgres NULL/JSONB representation.
   - `TestAxonStore_RealPostgres_TimestampPrecision` — RFC3339Nano roundtrips with Postgres `TIMESTAMPTZ` (nanosecond truncation behavior documented).
   - `TestAxonStore_RealPostgres_SchemaVersionDefault` — beads with `schema_version` absent default to v1 on read (TD-027 §11.4).

3. CI job: `.github/workflows/integration-axon.yml` runs the real-wire tests on a Postgres service container. Gated to manual trigger initially (Postgres setup is slow); converted to nightly once stable. Not on every PR.

4. Documentation: `cli/internal/bead/integration/axon/README.md` explains how to run locally (`docker-compose up postgres-test` + `go test -tags=integration ./internal/bead/integration/axon/...`).

# NON-SCOPE
- Production Lakebase setup — separate ops/infra concern.
- Performance benchmarks — separate work; this bead is correctness.
- Migrating the existing conformance suite to use real Postgres — would slow every PR. The httptest-served suite stays as the fast inner-loop test; real-wire is a gated outer-loop verification.

# Acceptance criteria

1. New package `cli/internal/bead/integration/axon/` with at least 7 real-wire tests listed above.
2. Tests run against a real Postgres instance (testcontainer or service container in CI). Test infrastructure documented in package README.
3. Build tag `//go:build integration` separates these tests from the default test run. `go test ./...` (no tag) doesn't run them; `go test -tags=integration ./internal/bead/integration/axon/...` does.
4. CI workflow `.github/workflows/integration-axon.yml` provisioned. Initial trigger: `workflow_dispatch` (manual). Validation: at least one successful run end-to-end before merge.
5. The full parameterized conformance suite from `ddx-958b8fc3` runs against the real Postgres backend (re-using the same test cases). Mock vs. real-wire produces identical pass/fail signal for every test.
6. Real-wire-specific findings documented as new tests (concurrency CAS, native heartbeat update via Postgres logs, transaction isolation, NULL/JSONB handling, timestamp precision, schema_version defaults).
7. Docs link added to TD-027 §28.4 (downstream acceptance) once landed.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

15. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","created_at":"2026-05-11T17:47:26.192982504Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-c6317784","depends_on_id":"ddx-05591bfd","type":"depends_on","created_at":"2026-05-12T19:40:28Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-c6317784","depends_on_id":"ddx-215b08a2","type":"depends_on","created_at":"2026-05-12T19:40:28Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-c6317784","depends_on_id":"ddx-494685af","type":"depends_on","created_at":"2026-05-12T19:40:28Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-c6317784","depends_on_id":"ddx-8e729130","type":"depends_on","created_at":"2026-05-12T19:40:28Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-c6317784","depends_on_id":"ddx-0f9915fd","type":"depends_on","created_at":"2026-05-12T19:40:28Z"}],"description":"# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel errors.
4. `cli/internal/bead/context.go` — typed accessors `WithIdentity`/`IdentityFromContext`/`WithTrace`/`TraceFromContext`. Package docstring per TD-027 §9 enumerating allowed ctx values.
5. `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` (expanded) — declare `BeadInitializer`, `BeadReader`, `BeadLifecycle` (Create + Apply only at this stage), `BeadEventReader`, `BeadEventWriter` interfaces. All ctx-first. Event interfaces are declarations only in this bead; no `*Store` event compile-time assertions until E. Composite `Backend` is not added yet (other sub-interfaces missing until per-concern beads land). `OperationApplier` optional interface declared. `RawBackend` retained with new docstring warning new backends off whole-corpus shape.

## Modify *Store

6. Add `*Store.Apply(ctx, id, op Operation) error` method per TD-027 §7.2: type-asserts wrapped `RawBackend` to `OperationApplier` and delegates; otherwise generic `WithLock + ReadAll + op.Apply + WriteAll`.
7. Add ctx as first parameter to `*Store.Init`, `ReadAll`, `ReadAllFiltered`, `Get`, `GetWithArchive`, `Create`, `Update`, `Close`, plus `GenID`. Do not change `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind` here; E owns event ctx threading.

## Modify JSONLBackend

8. Add `JSONLBackend.Apply(ctx, id, op Operation) error` — satisfies `OperationApplier`. Empty type-switch + generic fallback (the per-concern Claim bead adds the `SetClaimHeartbeat`/`ClearClaimHeartbeat` sidecar-optimization cases later).

## Caller migration (ctx threading only — scoped to foundational non-event methods)

9. Update every caller in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, `cli/internal/escalation/`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/`, `cli/internal/attemptmetrics/`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/`, `cli/internal/exec/`, `cli/internal/agent/try/` that calls the foundational non-event methods (Init/ReadAll/Get/Create/Update/Close/GenID) to pass `ctx` through. Use `cmd.Context()` for cobra commands; existing request ctx for HTTP/GraphQL handlers; `context.Background()` only where no ctx is naturally available.

# NON-SCOPE
- Event method ctx threading (`AppendEvent`, `Events`, `EventsByKind`) — E bead `ddx-f39b41b3`.
- Helper packages (`ops/<concern>/`) — per-concern beads handle these.
- Removing any `*Store` workflow methods (Claim, Heartbeat, TransitionLifecycle, RequestCancel, SetExecutionCooldown, etc.) — per-concern beads handle removal as they migrate callers.
- AxonStore implementation — separate bead `ddx-9c5bca8f`.
- Caller narrowing from `*bead.Store` field types to `bead.Backend` interface — happens in BL bead `ddx-74452926` after per-concern beads complete.
- Module boundary lockdown (relocation to `internal/storage/`) — BL bead.
- Schema-versioning migration framework — deferred until first breaking change.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:315:{"acceptance":"1. Add or update TestLifecycleProposedToOpenAppendsTriaged to prove operator status update proposed -> open leaves durable triaged/operator-acceptance evidence visible to the readiness loop.
2. Add TestPreClaimIntakeOperatorOverridePreventsRepeatParking: seed a bead that was parked for readiness, promote it proposed -> open, run the same readiness finding again, and assert status remains open or proceeds to claim instead of proposed.
3. Add TestPreClaimIntakeChangedBeadInvalidatesOverride: after the operator promotion, mutate prompt-relevant bead text/AC and prove a new hard operator_required finding may park with a new fingerprint.
4. Production wiring is covered: the override check runs before every pre-claim ParkToProposed call in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go, not only in a test helper.

After an operator reviews a proposed bead and promotes it back to open, pre-claim readiness can evaluate the same finding again and park the bead back to proposed. That creates a downgrade loop and contradicts the operator intent to keep work moving.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-027-bead-collection-abstraction.md:70-75 already defines proposed -> open as operator-driven triaged, but cli/internal/bead/store.go:587-628 status transitions mutate lifecycle metadata without necessarily appending a durable triaged event that readiness can query.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1207-1226 unconditionally parks terminal readiness findings without checking for a later operator triage/acceptance.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2784-2815 stores intake.blocked without a stable fingerprint, so even if an operator override exists the loop cannot tell whether the current readiness finding is the same one.

PROPOSED FIX
- Reuse the existing TD-027 triaged semantics for proposed -> open. Ensure operator promotion records a durable signal: either a triaged event or an explicit operator-accepted marker attached to the status transition event body.
- Add a stable readiness finding fingerprint for parkable intake findings. Include at least the decision class/rule, normalized reason, and a hash of prompt-relevant bead fields used to determine whether the bead changed since operator acceptance.
- Before parking an open bead for pre-claim readiness, check whether a later proposed -> open operator acceptance covers the same fingerprint. If yes, append/emit override-honored evidence and proceed instead of parking.
- If prompt-relevant fields changed after the operator acceptance, allow readiness to re-evaluate and park with a new fingerprint.

NON-SCOPE
- No broad WARN/BLOCK policy changes; those are in ddx-91898f19.
- No new event vocabulary unless the spec/observability bead authorizes it.
- No changes to closed/reopen semantics for closed -> open.
